Premise 3: Pet:rabbit is not to the left of Hobby:singing. So rabbit's position is >= singing's position.

Premise 4: Nationality:australian is left or right of Nationality:pakistani. So they are not adjacent? Wait, no. It just says australian is either left or right of pakistani. So they can be in any positions as long as one is left of the other.

Premise 5: Nationality:japanese == Food:kiwi. So wherever Japanese is, their food is kiwi.

Premise 8: Pet:snake is right of Food:plum. So snake's position is higher than plum's.

Premise 10: Food:avocado is between Pet:lizard and Food:plum. So avocado is in a position that's between lizard and plum. So if lizard is at position x and plum at y, avocado is between x and y. So either lizard < avocado < plum or plum < avocado < lizard. But since avocado is between them, the order would be lizard, avocado, plum or plum, avocado, lizard. But since positions are left to right, the first case is possible if lizard is left of plum, or the second if plum is left of lizard. But avocado is between them, so the positions must be such that avocado is in between.

Premise 11: Food:avocado == Nationality:pakistani. So the person with avocado as food is Pakistani.

Premise 12: Hobby:dancing is on the far right. So position 5 is dancing.

Premise 13: Food:plum is between Hobby:gardening and Hobby:singing. So gardening and singing are on either side of plum. So gardening < plum < singing or singing < plum < gardening. But since gardening is left of video-games (from premise 1), and dancing is at 5, maybe we can figure out the order.
